title: Taking back $30 million I-81 neighborhood grant is ‘morally indefensible’ (Your Letters)
summary: Opinion letter advocates for restoration of a $30 million federal grant for 15th Ward neighborhood revitalization following I-81 viaduct removal, after House Republicans voted to rescind it. Writer frames the funding as essential to address historical community displacement and inequity, characterizing the rescission as 'morally indefensible' and calling for city leadership intervention.
department_refs: Department of Parks, Recreation and Youth Programs

political_signal.description: Constituent advocacy calling for municipal leadership to actively oppose federal funding rescission and restore $30M grant for neighborhood revitalization; frames issue as equity and justice priority.

processing_notes: Dollar amount corrected from '$30' to '$30 million' based on article content
Article is opinion/letters section, not official policy announcement
Federal funding at risk—intergovernmental coordination likely required for advocacy response
